description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: test, doc, and deploy. Add four test jobs named test:2.7, test:3.4, test:3.5, and test:3.6, each using the corresponding python:2.7, python:3.4, python:3.5, and python:3.6 Docker images; all run the commands `git --no-pager diff --check $(git log --oneline | tail -1 | cut -f1 -d' ')`, `python -V`, `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `flake8`, and `py.test -vv --cov-config .coveragerc --cov geolink_formatter tests`. The test:3.6 job also caches the pip cache directory `$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.pip-cache` with key `$CI_PIPELINE_ID`. Add a codequality job (allow_failure true) that uses the docker:stable image with a docker:stable-dind service, sets `DOCKER_DRIVER=overlay2`, exports `SP_VERSION` derived from `$CI_SERVER_VERSION`, and runs `docker run --env SOURCE_CODE="$PWD" --volume "$PWD":/code --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock "registry.gitlab.com/gitlab-org/security-products/codequality:$SP_VERSION" /code`; it stores `codeclimate.json` as an artifact. Add a sphinx job in the doc stage using python:3.6, caching the same pip cache, producing an artifact at `doc/build/html`, running `pip install -r requirements.txt` and `sphinx-versioning build doc/source doc/build/html`, and only executing on tags and the master branch. Finally, add a pages job in the deploy stage using python:3.6, caching the pip cache, depending on the sphinx job, moving `doc/build/html/` to `public/` as the pages artifact, and also limited to tags and master. Define a global variable `PIP_CACHE_DIR=$CI_PROJECT_DIR/.pip-cache` and set the image for the default job to python:3.6.
